mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reflects tightening labor market conditions that boost household income and consumption, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining UNRATE periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as discount rates adjust.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a contraction in real consumer spending signals supply-demand imbalance that erodes corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Within a high inflation regime, a monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating economic fundamentals that compress equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns in rising UNRATE periods are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, or the conditional Sharpe ratio fails to exceed -0.5 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strengthening consumer confidence and credit availability, which supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Rank IC for HOUST and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02, or the sign of the forward return distribution does not align with HOUST increases in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 98.0, it signals supply chain contraction and capacity utilization stress, triggering negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the predictive log-odds ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with stable unemployment indicates a soft landing policy stance that sustains equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to generate a positive predictive coefficient in a multivariate regression, or 20-day forward returns do not outperform baseline when both FEDFUNDS rises and UNRATE remains flat.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, accelerating real consumer spending directly boosts retail and consumer discretionary earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns fail to correlate positively with RSAFS growth when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the conditional correlation coefficient drops below 0.15 over a rolling 12-month window.
